message:
fix(storage): validate_fts_query short-circuits on first match instead of full COUNT(*) scan (ft-76d9i)

Pre-fix shape was a foot-gun:

  SELECT COUNT(*) FROM output_segments_fts
  WHERE output_segments_fts MATCH ?1 LIMIT 1

`LIMIT 1` caps OUTPUT rows, not the work the planner does to compute
the aggregate. `COUNT(*)` returns one row regardless, so the LIMIT is
a no-op and the planner enumerates every matching FTS5 entry. Then
search_fts_with_snippets ran the same MATCH again for the real
BM25/snippet query — every lexical search paid for two full scans
and held the read connection twice as long under load. For broad
terms ("error", "warn") this is a noticeable read-side win.

Fix: switch the syntax probe to

  SELECT 1 FROM output_segments_fts
  WHERE output_segments_fts MATCH ?1 LIMIT 1

The planner short-circuits on the first matching rowid (or returns
no-rows for an empty match set). Empty-match-set semantics are
preserved by promoting `QueryReturnedNoRows` to `Ok(())` — pre-fix
returned Ok with count=0, post-fix returns Ok with no rows; both
mean "syntactically valid, no matches", which is what callers want
from a syntax probe.

FTS5 syntax errors still surface at execution time (xFilter), not
prepare time, so we can't drop the probe entirely — but we can stop
it after the first match.

Two new regression tests:
  - ft_76d9i_validate_fts_query_accepts_empty_match_set: pin that
    no-rows is treated as syntactically valid (was implicit before;
    now an explicit Ok arm).
  - ft_76d9i_validate_fts_query_accepts_well_formed_matching_query:
    pin the happy path through 4 query shapes (simple word, prefix
    `*`, AND, OR) so a future refactor can't regress validation
    even when the index is non-empty.

Verification: 14 existing fts_search_* tests + 2 new ft_76d9i_*
tests pass against /tmp/ft-pane0-76d9i-target. No call-site or API
change — fix is purely the SQL shape inside `validate_fts_query`.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
